core_mechanism: inference of a population's growth law from the distribution of internal branching (coalescence) times in the reconstructed genealogy of a continuous-time birth-death branching process, where a saturating (logistic) rate shifts that distribution through distinct unimodal/bimodal regimes versus the exponential case
brief_reason: The abstract studies continuous-time birth-death models whose inferred phylogenetic trees yield internal branching-time distributions that qualitatively change (unimodal to bimodal to recent-dominated) depending on whether growth is exponential or carrying-capacity-limited, making the branching-time statistic a signature of the underlying growth regime.
